claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children are you applying for Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit for my child. I have 1 child I'd like to apply for.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me gather the details I need to assess your eligibility.

**What is your child's name?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to ask you some questions about Alex to determine eligibility.

**How old is Alex currently?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. I need to check a few more important details about Alex's circumstances.

**Is Alex in full-time education or training (such as school, college, or an approved training scheme)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is just in regular school - he's 13, so just doing normal secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I appreciate you clarifying that. Just to confirm - when you say Alex is in "regular school" doing "normal secondary school," that means Alex is attending school full-time as a student, correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right - Alex attends secondary school full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for clarifying. I just need to confirm one more important detail.

**Are you a British citizen, or do you have a right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Actually, no - I don't live in the UK. I'm abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important information. Before I can make a final assessment, I need to understand your situation a bit more.

**Do you have a UK address, or are you permanently residing outside the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm permanently residing outside the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. I need to check the eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate information about your situation. Let me review the official guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I need to clarify one important point:

The UK government states that you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and **you live in the UK**. 

Since you've indicated that you are permanently residing outside the UK, and there's a section about "Living abroad" which mentions you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you go to live in certain countries or if you're a Crown servant, I need to ask:

**Are you a Crown servant (such as a diplomat, civil servant, or military personnel posted abroad)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can now make an assessment.

The UK Child Benefit rules state that you normally qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 **and you live in the UK**. The guidance indicates that you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you live abroad in certain specific circumstances, such as if you're a Crown servant - which does not apply to your situation.

Since you are permanently residing outside the UK and are not a Crown servant, you do not meet the basic residency requirement for Child Benefit eligibility.

Let me now provide my formal outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Result: You are not eligible for Child Benefit**

Unfortunately, based on the information you've provided, you do not meet the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. While Alex meets the age and education requirements (being 13 and in full-time secondary education), the fundamental requirement for Child Benefit is that **you must live in the UK**.

Since you are permanently residing outside the UK and are not a Crown servant, you fall outside the eligibility criteria. The UK government does allow limited exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants or diplomats), but these do not apply to your situation.

If your circumstances change and you move back to the UK, you would then be able to apply for Child Benefit for Alex.

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old and in full-time secondary education, which would normally meet the age and education requirements for Child Benefit eligibility. However, the claimant does not meet the fundamental residency requirement. Child Benefit eligibility requires that the claimant lives in the UK. The claimant has confirmed they are permanently residing outside the UK and are not a Crown servant. According to the eligibility rules, you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you live abroad in certain specific circumstances (such as being a Crown servant), which do not apply here. Therefore, Alex is not eligible.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit because they do not meet the core residency requirement of living in the UK. Although Alex meets the age and education criteria for eligibility (being 13 and in full-time secondary education), the claimant's permanent residence outside the UK means they fall outside the scope of Child Benefit eligibility. The rules do allow for limited exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants), but the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions."

According to the eligibility rules, you may only be able to get Child Benefit if you live abroad in certain specific circumstances (such as being a Crown servant), which do not apply here. Therefore, Alex is not eligible.

The rules do allow for limited exceptions for those living abroad (such as Crown servants), but the claimant does not meet any of these exceptions."
